rulings.Case Title: DONALD GAUGER et al VS. ALCATEL LUCENT et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion And Motion To Strike Portions Of Plaintiffs¿ Complaint
rulings.Rulings: ON CALENDAR FOR THURSDAY, JUNE 24, 2010 IN DEPARTMENT 220 AT 9:30 AM, LINE 1. DEFENDANT UNITED TECHNOLOGIES CORPORATION'S Motion To Strike Portions Of Plaintiffs' Complaint. Defendant United Technologies¿ motion to strike is denied. The plaintiffs allege in paragraphs 22 -25 of their complaint that defendant was aware of the hazards of asbestos and nevertheless sold its asbestos-containing products to the public. = (220/HEK)

rulings.Case Title: DONALD GAUGER et al VS. ALCATEL LUCENT et al
rulings.Rulings: ON CALENDAR FOR THURSDAY, JUNE 24, 2010 IN DEPARTMENT 220 AT 9:30 AM, LINE 1. DEFENDANT UNITED TECHNOLOGIES CORPORATION'S DEMURRER TO FIRST AMENDED COMPLAINT. Defendant United Technologies¿ demurrer is sustained in part and overruled in part. The demurrer is overruled as to the 1st, 2nd. 8th and 9th causes of action because plaintiffs specified the products they allege decedent was exposed to in a manner consistent with the pleading requirements of Bockrath v. Aldrich Chemical Co., Inc. (1999) 21 Cal.4th 71. The demurrer is sustained with 10 days leave to amend as to the 3rd and 4th causes of action because plaintiffs failed to allege the names of the persons who made the allegedly fraudulent representations, their authority to speak, to whom they spoke, what they said or wrote, and when it was said or written. (Tarmann v. State Farm Mut. Auto. Ins. Co. (1991) 2 Cal.App.4th 153) = (220/HEK)

rulings.Case Title: MARIA A. NAVARRO et al VS. ADVOCATE MINES LIMITED et al
rulings.Rulings: ON CALENDAR FOR THURSDAY, JUNE 24, 2010 IN DEPARTMENT 220 AT 9:30 AM, LINE 3. DEFENDANT HAJOCA CORPORATION DEMURRER TO FIRST AMENDED COMPLAINT. Defendant Hajoca Corporation¿s demurrer is sustained with 10 days leave to amend. The demurrer is sustained as to the first and second causes of action because the complaint fails to specify the ¿asbestos-containing products¿ plaintiffs allege Hajoca is responsible for as required by Bockrath v. Aldrich Chemical Co., Inc. (1999)21 Cal.4th 71. The demurrer is sustained as to the third and fourth causes of action because the complaint fails to allege the names of the persons who made the allegedly fraudulent representations, their authority to speak, to whom they spoke, what they said or wrote, and when it was said or written. (Tarmann v. State Farm Mut. Auto. Ins. Co. (1991) 2 Cal.App.4th 153)., The demurrer is sustained as to the ninth cause of action because plaintiff¿s loss of consortium claim is derivative of whatever claims decedent may have had. = (220/HEK)

rulings.Case Title: MARIA A. NAVARRO et al VS. ADVOCATE MINES LIMITED et al
rulings.Rulings: ON CALENDAR FOR THURSDAY, JUNE 24, 2010 IN DEPARTMENT 220 AT 9:30 AM, LINE 3. DEFENDANT AUTOZONE WEST, INC. MOTION TO STRIKE FIRST AMENDED COMPLAINT. Defendant Autozone West¿s motion to strike is denied. Plaintiffs adequately allege that defendant and its officers, directors and managers knew of the hazards of asbestos and concealed that information from the users of its products. While the wrongful death plaintiffs are not entitled to punitive damages, one of the plaintiffs is Maria Navarro, decedent¿s successor-in-interest, who may obtain punitive damages as an element of the survival claims. = (220/HEK)

rulings.Case Title: MARIA A. NAVARRO et al VS. ADVOCATE MINES LIMITED et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion And Motion To Strike Portions Of Plaintiffs First Amended Complaint For Survival Wrongful Death And Loss Of Consortium - Asbestos

DEFENDANT HAJOCA CORPORATION'S Motion To Strike Portions Of Plaintiffs First Amended Complaint For Survival Wrongful Death And Loss Of Consortium - Asbestos. Defendant Hajoca Corporation¿s motion to strike is moot in light of the ruling on its demurrer. = (220/HEK)

rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion And Motion To Dismiss For Failure To Prosecute
rulings.Rulings: ON CALENDAR FOR THURSDAY, JUNE 24, 2010 IN DEPARTMENT 220 AT 9:30 AM, LINE 4. DEFENDANT FLUOR CORPORATION'S Motion To Dismiss For Failure To Prosecute is granted. No opposition filed. A hearing is required unless counsel for Flour informs the plaintiffs of the tentative ruling and the plaintiffs tell defense counsel that they do not wish to contest it. = (220/HEK)
